candidates.title: High-pressure protection
candidates.meaning: The GMV6 outdoor unit has stopped after detecting system high-pressure protection conditions.
candidates.severity: Protection shutdown requiring qualified technician investigation

source.manualTitle: Gree GMV6 DC Inverter VRF Units Service Manual
source.section: 2.57 E1 Protection in Case of Too High Pressure
source.notes: Evidence extracted from the local manual intake file and requires engineering review before runtime promotion.
limitations: GMV6 outdoor units only.
Verify the exact installed model and controller display.
Staging candidate only; not a final diagnosis.
Qualified technician investigation is required.

likelyCauses: Abnormal pressure-sensor indication, restricted heat exchange or airflow, valve position, fan operation, or refrigerant condition requires review.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm identity and indication
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Confirm the installed GMV6 outdoor unit identity and record the displayed E1 code.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Equipment identity and E1 indication are confirmed.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Keep the candidate unconfirmed and verify the exact service manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Review pressure-protection conditions
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should compare measured pressure with sensor indication and inspect supported airflow, fan, valve, and heat-exchanger conditions while keeping protections active.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Measured and indicated conditions identify the next manual-supported review step.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop and escalate for engineering review.

requiredMeasurements.name: High-side pressure
requiredMeasurements.description: Record external gauge pressure and controller/sensor indication.

safetyNotes: Refrigerant-circuit, electrical, fan, and compressor checks require a qualified technician; keep all safety protections active.
promotionNotes: Engineering review and installed-model applicability confirmation are required before any runtime promotion.

diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Measured and indicated conditions identify the next manual-supported review step.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop and escalate for engineering review.

safetyNotes: Refrigerant-circuit, electrical, fan, and compressor checks require a qualified technician; keep all safety protections active.
promotionNotes: Engineering review and installed-model applicability confirmation are required before any runtime promotion.

candidates.title: High compressor discharge temperature protection
candidates.meaning: The GMV6 outdoor unit has stopped after detecting excessive compressor discharge temperature.
candidates.severity: Protection shutdown requiring qualified technician investigation

source.manualTitle: Gree GMV6 DC Inverter VRF Units Service Manual
source.section: 2.60 E4 Protection in Case of Too High Air Discharge Temperature of Compressor
source.notes: Evidence extracted from the local manual intake file and requires engineering review before runtime promotion.
limitations: GMV6 outdoor units only.
Verify the exact installed model and controller display.
Staging candidate only; not a final diagnosis.
Qualified technician investigation is required.

likelyCauses: Discharge-temperature sensing, valve operation, piping/refrigerant condition, or compressor operating condition requires review.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm identity and indication
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Confirm the installed GMV6 outdoor unit identity and record the displayed E4 code.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Equipment identity and E4 indication are confirmed.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Keep the candidate unconfirmed and verify the exact service manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Review discharge-temperature conditions
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should record discharge-temperature indications and inspect supported sensor, valve, piping, and refrigerant conditions while keeping protections active.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Recorded conditions identify the next manual-supported review step.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop and escalate for engineering review.

requiredMeasurements.name: Compressor discharge temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Record controller and supported sensor temperature indications.

safetyNotes: Electrical, refrigerant-circuit, and compressor checks require a qualified technician; keep all safety protections active.
promotionNotes: Engineering review and installed-model applicability confirmation are required before any runtime promotion.

candidates.title: Poor main board of outdoor unit
candidates.meaning: The GMV6 outdoor unit reports an abnormal main-board chip read condition.
candidates.severity: Service fault requiring qualified technician investigation

source.manualTitle: Gree GMV6 DC Inverter VRF Units Service Manual
source.section: 2.61 F0 Poor Main Board of Outdoor Unit
source.notes: Evidence extracted from the local manual intake file and requires engineering review before runtime promotion.
limitations: GMV6 outdoor units only.
Verify the exact installed model and controller display.
Staging candidate only; not a final diagnosis.
Qualified technician investigation is required.

likelyCauses: Outdoor main-board address, memory, or clock chip condition requires qualified review.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm identity and indication
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Confirm the installed GMV6 outdoor unit identity and record the displayed F0 code.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Equipment identity and F0 indication are confirmed.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Keep the candidate unconfirmed and verify the exact service manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect main-board condition
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect board condition, connectors, and supported chip-read diagnostics while keeping protections active.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: The board condition is documented for engineering review.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop and escalate for engineering review.

requiredMeasurements.name: Outdoor main-board supply voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Record the supported board supply voltage before conclusion.

safetyNotes: Electrical and main-board checks require a qualified technician; isolate power according to the exact service procedure and keep safety protections active.
promotionNotes: Engineering review and installed-model applicability confirmation are required before any runtime promotion.

candidates.title: High-pressure sensor fault
candidates.meaning: The GMV6 outdoor unit reports a high-pressure sensor detection-circuit value outside the expected range.
candidates.severity: Service fault requiring qualified technician investigation

source.manualTitle: Gree GMV6 DC Inverter VRF Units Service Manual
source.notes: Evidence extracted from the local manual intake file and requires engineering review before runtime promotion.
limitations: GMV6 outdoor units only.
Verify the exact installed model and controller display.
Staging candidate only; not a final diagnosis.
Qualified technician investigation is required.

likelyCauses: High-pressure sensor contact, wiring, sensor, or outdoor main-board detection circuit requires review.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm identity and indication
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Confirm the installed GMV6 outdoor unit identity and record the displayed F1 code.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Equipment identity and F1 indication are confirmed.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Keep the candidate unconfirmed and verify the exact service manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Compare sensor and measured pressure
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect supported sensor connections and compare sensor indication with measured high-side pressure while keeping protections active.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Sensor, wiring, and pressure observations are documented.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop and escalate for engineering review.

requiredMeasurements.name: High-pressure sensor indication and gauge pressure
requiredMeasurements.description: Record sensor/controller indication and external gauge pressure.

safetyNotes: Electrical and refrigerant-pressure checks require a qualified technician; keep all safety protections active.
promotionNotes: Engineering review and installed-model applicability confirmation are required before any runtime promotion.

candidates.title: Indoor unit engineering serial-number conflict
candidates.meaning: The GMV6 system reports inconsistent or conflicting indoor-unit project code settings.
candidates.severity: Commissioning fault requiring qualified technician review

source.manualTitle: Gree GMV6 DC Inverter VRF Units Service Manual
source.section: 2.31 C5 Indoor Unit Engineering SN Conflict
source.notes: Evidence extracted from the local manual intake file and requires engineering review before runtime promotion.
limitations: GMV6 outdoor-system commissioning context only.
Verify the exact installed indoor-unit family and controller display.
Staging candidate only; not a final diagnosis.
Qualified technician investigation is required.

likelyCauses: Indoor-unit engineering serial-number or project-code settings require commissioning review.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm system identity and indication
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Confirm the GMV6 system identity and record the displayed C5 code and affected indoor units.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: System identity, C5 indication, and affected units are recorded.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Keep the candidate unconfirmed and verify the exact service manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Review commissioning settings
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should compare supported indoor-unit project-code or engineering serial-number settings against the commissioned system record.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Conflicting or inconsistent settings are documented for engineering review.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop and escalate for commissioning review.

requiredMeasurements.name: Indoor-unit project code inventory
requiredMeasurements.description: Record the displayed/project-code settings for affected indoor units.

safetyNotes: Commissioning and electrical/control checks require a qualified technician; keep all safety protections active.
promotionNotes: Engineering review and installed-system applicability confirmation are required before any runtime promotion.

candidates.title: Abnormal communication of converter
candidates.meaning: The GMV6 debugging/status indication table identifies abnormal converter communication.
candidates.severity: Debugging status requiring qualified technician review

source.manualTitle: Gree GMV6 DC Inverter VRF Units Service Manual
source.section: Debugging/status indication table: C7 Abnormal communication of converter
source.notes: Table evidence extracted from the local manual intake file; a detailed troubleshooting section was not identified and requires engineering review before runtime promotion.
limitations: GMV6 outdoor debugging/status table evidence only.
Detailed troubleshooting section still requires review.
Verify the exact installed model and controller display.
Staging candidate only; not a final diagnosis.
Qualified technician investigation is required.

likelyCauses: Converter communication path, connectors, wiring, supply, or control-board condition requires qualified review; detailed manual flow remains unverified.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm identity and indication
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Confirm the installed GMV6 outdoor unit identity and record the displayed C7 debugging/status indication.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Equipment identity and C7 indication are confirmed.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Keep the candidate unconfirmed and verify the exact service manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Document communication context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should document converter communication status, supported connector/wiring observations, and relevant supply indications while keeping protections active.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Communication context is documented for detailed engineering review.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop and escalate for engineering review.

requiredMeasurements.name: Converter communication and supply status
requiredMeasurements.description: Record supported communication status and relevant supply indications.

safetyNotes: Electrical, converter, and control-board checks require a qualified technician; keep all safety protections active.
promotionNotes: Detailed troubleshooting evidence and engineering review are required before any runtime promotion.
